key_insight: A pocket-conditioned diffusion framework can generate 3D ligands that better balance binding and developability, not just affinity.
clinical_relevance: This could accelerate early-stage discovery of cancer-relevant and other therapeutic hits by producing molecules with experimentally validated target activity and improved ADMET profiles.
method: msPRL learns multi-scale protein pocket representations
conDitar uses pocket-conditioned diffusion to generate ligands
paOPT optimizes generated molecules for developability during generation
evidence_signal: On a curated human disease-target benchmark, conDitar achieved an average binding score of -8.85 kcal/mol, and conDitar-dev improved five ADMET properties by up to 73%; experimental testing of PD-L1 and CSF1R hits showed micromolar to nanomolar activity.
watch_item: Results are promising but depend on benchmark quality, target selection, and experimental follow-up, so broader generalization and synthesis feasibility still need validation.
paper_title: Generating Developable 3D Molecules via Pocket-Conditioned Diffusion and Property-Aware Optimization
